home.title: Cuyahoga Valley Countryside Conservancy | Heritage & working lands archive
home.description: An impact driven organization to support agriculture and stewardship

hero_image.alt: A working landscape in the Cuyahoga Valley: crop fields, hedgerows, and farmsteads under an open sky
hero_image.caption: A working landscape

content.headline: Connecting people to food in and beyond Cuyahoga Valley National Park

intro_panels.note_html: Who we are · Meet the board members · Visit the farms
intro_panels.heading: Who we are
body: The Cuyahoga Valley Countryside Conservancy (CVCC) is a small nonprofit serving the Cuyahoga Valley in Ohio (and beyond). We are a group of individuals dedicated to supporting area farms, promoting sustainable agriculture, educating the public about sustainable practices, and connecting our community with healthy, local food.

intro_panels.note_html: What we do · Continue on History
intro_panels.heading: What We do
body: Originally created to support the Countryside Initiative, Cuyahoga Valley National Parks' program to rehabilitate farmland within the park, the CVCC continues to support the Initiative farms and has begun expanding it's support to include other local farms, farmers, and farmers markets.

intro_panels.note_html: How you can help · Support the mission
intro_panels.heading: How you can help
body: Your donations will be used to:
Support infrastructure projects for farms both within the National Park and surrounding areas.
Provide grants to local farms and farmers emphasizing sustainable agriculture and education.
Connect local people to healthy food and a supportive community.

donate_callout.body: Help sustain preservation work, farmer training, and year-round public programming through the donation page.

history.title: History | Cuyahoga Valley Countryside Conservancy
history.description: Chronology-first archival history for the Countryside Conservancy and the agricultural life of the Cuyahoga Valley.

content.heading: History
intro_html: This page is now rendered directly from assets/docs/timeline.json. Periods provide the conceptual frame, but the events remain the primary record: dated stops, oral histories, images, source trails, and place-based references tied to the changing agricultural life of the valley.
Edit the timeline dataset to update this page’s periods, chronology, media, and documentation without rewriting the markup below.
content.summary_note_html: Events are sorted numerically by start.parts and grouped under their assigned period. Optional media, transcripts, source links, map links, and file references appear only when present in the JSON.

content.annual_note_html: Upcoming cards, monthly schedule summaries, and section notes are rendered at build time from one source: assets/docs/happenings.json.

content.detail_note: Section summaries below preserve the source dataset’s annual framing while keeping the public page readable without JavaScript.
